1→package pub.hackers.android.ui.components
     2→
     3→import androidx.compose.foundation.BorderStroke
     4→import androidx.compose.foundation.clickable
     5→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Arrangement
     6→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Column
     7→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Row
     8→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Spacer
     9→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxWidth
    10→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
    11→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.padding
    12→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.size
    13→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.width
    14→import androidx.compose.foundation.shape.CircleShape
    15→import androidx.compose.foundation.shape.RoundedCornerShape
    16→import androidx.compose.material.icons.Icons
    17→import androidx.compose.material.icons.filled.Repeat
    18→import androidx.compose.material.icons.outlined.ChatBubbleOutline
    19→import androidx.compose.material.icons.outlined.Favorite
    20→import androidx.compose.material.icons.outlined.FormatQuote
    21→import androidx.compose.material.icons.outlined.Repeat
    22→import androidx.compose.material3.Card
    23→import androidx.compose.material3.CardDefaults
    24→import androidx.compose.material3.Icon
    25→import androidx.compose.material3.IconButton
    26→import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
    27→import androidx.compose.material3.Surface
    28→import androidx.compose.material3.Text
    29→import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
    30→import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
    31→import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
    32→import androidx.compose.ui.draw.clip
    33→import androidx.compose.ui.layout.ContentScale
    34→import androidx.compose.ui.res.stringResource
    35→import androidx.compose.ui.text.font.FontWeight
    36→import androidx.compose.ui.text.style.TextOverflow
    37→import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
    38→import coil.compose.AsyncImage
    39→import pub.hackers.android.R
    40→import pub.hackers.android.domain.model.Post
    41→import java.time.Duration
    42→import java.time.Instant
    43→
    44→@Composable
    45→fun PostCard(
    46→    post: Post,
    47→    onClick: () -> Unit,
    48→    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit,
    49→    onReplyClick: (() -> Unit)? = null,
    50→    onShareClick: (() -> Unit)? = null,
    51→    onQuotedPostClick: ((String) -> Unit)? = null,
    52→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier
    53→) {
    54→    val displayPost = post.sharedPost ?: post
    55→    val isRepost = post.sharedPost != null
    56→
    57→    Card(
    58→        modifier = modifier
    59→            .fillMaxWidth()
    60→            .clickable(onClick = onClick),
    61→        colors = CardDefaults.cardColors(
    62→            containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surface
    63→        ),
    64→        elevation = CardDefaults.cardElevation(defaultElevation = 0.dp)
    65→    ) {
    66→        Column(
    67→            modifier = Modifier.padding(12.dp)
    68→        ) {
    69→            if (isRepost) {
    70→                Row(
    71→                    verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically,
    72→                    modifier = Modifier.padding(bottom = 8.dp)
    73→                ) {
    74→                    Icon(
    75→                        imageVector = Icons.Filled.Repeat,
    76→                        contentDescription = null,
    77→                        tint = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.secondary,
    78→                        modifier = Modifier.size(16.dp)
    79→                    )
    80→                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(4.dp))
    81→                    Text(
    82→                        text = "${post.actor.name ?: post.actor.handle} ${stringResource(R.string.share)}d",
    83→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
    84→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.secondary
    85→                    )
    86→                }
    87→            }
    88→
    89→            Row(
    90→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()
    91→            ) {
    92→                AsyncImage(
    93→                    model = displayPost.actor.avatarUrl,
    94→                    contentDescription = "Avatar",
    95→                    modifier = Modifier
    96→                        .size(48.dp)
    97→                        .clip(CircleShape)
    98→                        .clickable { onProfileClick(displayPost.actor.handle) },
    99→                    contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   100→                )
   101→
   102→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(12.dp))
   103→
   104→                Column(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)) {
   105→                    Row(
   106→                        verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
   107→                    ) {
   108→                        Text(
   109→                            text = displayPost.actor.name ?: displayPost.actor.handle,
   110→                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
   111→                            fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold,
   112→                            maxLines = 1,
   113→                            overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis,
   114→                            modifier = Modifier
   115→                                .weight(1f, fill = false)
   116→                                .clickable { onProfileClick(displayPost.actor.handle) }
   117→                        )
   118→                        Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(4.dp))
   119→                        Text(
   120→                            text = formatRelativeTime(displayPost.published),
   121→                            style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   122→                            color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   123→                        )
   124→                    }
   125→
   126→                    Text(
   127→                        text = "@${displayPost.actor.handle}",
   128→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   129→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   130→                        maxLines = 1,
   131→                        overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis
   132→                    )
   133→                }
   134→            }
   135→
   136→            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   137→
   138→            displayPost.name?.let { title ->
   139→                Text(
   140→                    text = title,
   141→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
   142→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold,
   143→                    modifier = Modifier.padding(bottom = 4.dp)
   144→                )
   145→            }
   146→
   147→            HtmlContent(
   148→                html = displayPost.content,
   149→                maxLines = 10,
   150→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   151→                onMentionClick = onProfileClick
   152→            )
   153→
   154→            if (displayPost.media.isNotEmpty()) {
   155→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   156→                MediaGrid(media = displayPost.media)
   157→            }
   158→
   159→            if (displayPost.quotedPost != null) {
   160→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   161→                QuotedPostPreview(
   162→                    post = displayPost.quotedPost!!,
   163→                    onClick = { onQuotedPostClick?.invoke(displayPost.quotedPost!!.id) },
   164→                    onProfileClick = onProfileClick
   165→                )
   166→            }
   167→
   168→            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(12.dp))
   169→
   170→            EngagementBar(
   171→                post = displayPost,
   172→                onReplyClick = onReplyClick,
   173→                onShareClick = onShareClick
   174→            )
   175→        }
   176→    }
   177→}
   178→
   179→@Composable
   180→private fun EngagementBar(
   181→    post: Post,
   182→    onReplyClick: (() -> Unit)?,
   183→    onShareClick: (() -> Unit)?
   184→) {
   185→    Row(
   186→        modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   187→        horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceBetween
   188→    ) {
   189→        EngagementButton(
   190→            icon = Icons.Outlined.ChatBubbleOutline,
   191→            count = post.engagementStats.replies,
   192→            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.replies),
   193→            onClick = onReplyClick
   194→        )
   195→
   196→        EngagementButton(
   197→            icon = if (post.viewerHasShared) Icons.Filled.Repeat else Icons.Outlined.Repeat,
   198→            count = post.engagementStats.shares,
   199→            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.shares),
   200→            onClick = onShareClick,
   201→            isActive = post.viewerHasShared
   202→        )
   203→
   204→        EngagementButton(
   205→            icon = Icons.Outlined.Favorite,
   206→            count = post.engagementStats.reactions,
   207→            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.reactions),
   208→            onClick = null
   209→        )
   210→
   211→        EngagementButton(
   212→            icon = Icons.Outlined.FormatQuote,
   213→            count = post.engagementStats.quotes,
   214→            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.quotes),
   215→            onClick = null
   216→        )
   217→    }
   218→}
   219→
   220→@Composable
   221→private fun EngagementButton(
   222→    icon: androidx.compose.ui.graphics.vector.ImageVector,
   223→    count: Int,
   224→    contentDescription: String,
   225→    onClick: (() -> Unit)?,
   226→    isActive: Boolean = false
   227→) {
   228→    Row(
   229→        verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
   230→    ) {
   231→        IconButton(
   232→            onClick = { onClick?.invoke() },
   233→            enabled = onClick != null
   234→        ) {
   235→            Icon(
   236→                imageVector = icon,
   237→                contentDescription = contentDescription,
   238→                tint = if (isActive) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   239→                modifier = Modifier.size(20.dp)
   240→            )
   241→        }
   242→        if (count > 0) {
   243→            Text(
   244→                text = formatCount(count),
   245→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   246→                color = if (isActive) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   247→            )
   248→        }
   249→    }
   250→}
   251→
   252→@Composable
   253→fun QuotedPostPreview(
   254→    post: Post,
   255→    onClick: () -> Unit,
   256→    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit
   257→) {
   258→    Surface(
   259→        modifier = Modifier
   260→            .fillMaxWidth()
   261→            .clickable(onClick = onClick),
   262→        shape = RoundedCornerShape(12.dp),
   263→        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surfaceVariant.copy(alpha = 0.5f),
   264→        border = BorderStroke(
   265→            1.dp,
   266→            MaterialTheme.colorScheme.outlineVariant
   267→        )
   268→    ) {
   269→        Column(
   270→            modifier = Modifier.padding(12.dp)
   271→        ) {
   272→            Row(
   273→                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
   274→            ) {
   275→                AsyncImage(
   276→                    model = post.actor.avatarUrl,
   277→                    contentDescription = null,
   278→                    modifier = Modifier
   279→                        .size(20.dp)
   280→                        .clip(CircleShape)
   281→                        .clickable { onProfileClick(post.actor.handle) },
   282→                    contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   283→                )
   284→
   285→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(8.dp))
   286→
   287→                Text(
   288→                    text = post.actor.name ?: post.actor.handle,
   289→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   290→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold,
   291→                    maxLines = 1,
   292→                    overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis,
   293→                    modifier = Modifier
   294→                        .weight(1f, fill = false)
   295→                        .clickable { onProfileClick(post.actor.handle) }
   296→                )
   297→
   298→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(4.dp))
   299→
   300→                Text(
   301→                    text = "@${post.actor.handle}",
   302→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   303→                    color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   304→                    maxLines = 1,
   305→                    overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis,
   306→                    modifier = Modifier.weight(1f, fill = false)
   307→                )
   308→            }
   309→
   310→            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   311→
   312→            post.name?.let { title ->
   313→                Text(
   314→                    text = title,
   315→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   316→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold,
   317→                    maxLines = 1,
   318→                    overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis,
   319→                    modifier = Modifier.padding(bottom = 4.dp)
   320→                )
   321→            }
   322→
   323→            HtmlContent(
   324→                html = post.content,
   325→                maxLines = 3,
   326→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   327→                onMentionClick = onProfileClick
   328→            )
   329→
   330→            if (post.media.isNotEmpty()) {
   331→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   332→                AsyncImage(
   333→                    model = post.media.first().url,
   334→                    contentDescription = post.media.first().alt,
   335→                    modifier = Modifier
   336→                        .fillMaxWidth()
   337→                        .height(120.dp)
   338→                        .clip(RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)),
   339→                    contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   340→                )
   341→            }
   342→        }
   343→    }
   344→}
   345→
   346→@Composable
   347→fun MediaGrid(media: List<pub.hackers.android.domain.model.Media>) {
   348→    when (media.size) {
   349→        1 -> {
   350→            AsyncImage(
   351→                model = media[0].url,
   352→                contentDescription = media[0].alt,
   353→                modifier = Modifier
   354→                    .fillMaxWidth()
   355→                    .height(200.dp)
   356→                    .clip(RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)),
   357→                contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   358→            )
   359→        }
   360→        2 -> {
   361→            Row(
   362→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   363→            ) {
   364→                media.forEach { item ->
   365→                    AsyncImage(
   366→                        model = item.url,
   367→                        contentDescription = item.alt,
   368→                        modifier = Modifier
   369→                            .weight(1f)
   370→                            .height(150.dp)
   371→                            .clip(RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)),
   372→                        contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   373→                    )
   374→                }
   375→            }
   376→        }
   377→        else -> {
   378→            Column(
   379→                verticalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   380→            ) {
   381→                Row(
   382→                    horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   383→                ) {
   384→                    media.take(2).forEach { item ->
   385→                        AsyncImage(
   386→                            model = item.url,
   387→                            contentDescription = item.alt,
   388→                            modifier = Modifier
   389→                                .weight(1f)
   390→                                .height(100.dp)
   391→                                .clip(RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)),
   392→                            contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   393→                        )
   394→                    }
   395→                }
   396→                if (media.size > 2) {
   397→                    Row(
   398→                        horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   399→                    ) {
   400→                        media.drop(2).take(2).forEach { item ->
   401→                            AsyncImage(
   402→                                model = item.url,
   403→                                contentDescription = item.alt,
   404→                                modifier = Modifier
   405→                                    .weight(1f)
   406→                                    .height(100.dp)
   407→                                    .clip(RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)),
   408→                                contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   409→                            )
   410→                        }
   411→                    }
   412→                }
   413→            }
   414→        }
   415→    }
   416→}
   417→
   418→
   419→private fun formatRelativeTime(instant: Instant): String {
   420→    val now = Instant.now()
   421→    val duration = Duration.between(instant, now)
   422→
   423→    return when {
   424→        duration.toMinutes() < 1 -> "now"
   425→        duration.toMinutes() < 60 -> "${duration.toMinutes()}m"
   426→        duration.toHours() < 24 -> "${duration.toHours()}h"
   427→        duration.toDays() < 7 -> "${duration.toDays()}d"
   428→        duration.toDays() < 30 -> "${duration.toDays() / 7}w"
   429→        duration.toDays() < 365 -> "${duration.toDays() / 30}mo"
   430→        else -> "${duration.toDays() / 365}y"
   431→    }
   432→}
   433→
   434→private fun formatCount(count: Int): String {
   435→    return when {
   436→        count < 1000 -> count.toString()
   437→        count < 1_000_000 -> String.format("%.1fK", count / 1000.0)
   438→        else -> String.format("%.1fM", count / 1_000_000.0)
   439→    }
   440→}
   441→

     2→
     3→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Box

     6→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.padding
     7→import androidx.compose.foundation.lazy.LazyColumn
     8→import androidx.compose.foundation.lazy.items
     9→import androidx.compose.foundation.lazy.rememberLazyListState
    10→import androidx.compose.material.icons.Icons
    11→import androidx.compose.material.icons.filled.Add
    12→import androidx.compose.material3.ExperimentalMaterial3Api
    13→import androidx.compose.material3.FloatingActionButton
    14→import androidx.compose.material3.HorizontalDivider
    15→import androidx.compose.material3.Icon
    16→import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
    17→import androidx.compose.material3.Scaffold
    18→import androidx.compose.material3.pulltorefresh.PullToRefreshBox
    19→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.CompactTopBar
    20→import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
    21→import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
    22→import androidx.compose.runtime.collectAsState
    23→import androidx.compose.runtime.derivedStateOf
    24→import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
    25→import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
    26→import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
    27→import androidx.compose.ui.res.stringResource
    28→import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
    29→import androidx.hilt.navigation.compose.hiltViewModel
    30→import pub.hackers.android.R
    31→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.ErrorMessage
    32→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.FullScreenLoading
    33→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.LoadingItem
    34→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.PostCard
    35→
    36→@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class)
    37→@Composable
    38→fun TimelineScreen(
    39→    onPostClick: (String) -> Unit,
    40→    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit,
    41→    onComposeClick: (String?) -> Unit,
    42→    viewModel: TimelineViewModel = hiltViewModel()
    43→) {
    44→    val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsState()
    45→    val listState = rememberLazyListState()
    46→
    47→    val shouldLoadMore by remember {
    48→        derivedStateOf {
    49→            val lastVisibleItem = listState.layoutInfo.visibleItemsInfo.lastOrNull()
    50→            lastVisibleItem != null && lastVisibleItem.index >= listState.layoutInfo.totalItemsCount - 3
    51→        }
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    LaunchedEffect(shouldLoadMore) {
    55→        if (shouldLoadMore && uiState.hasNextPage && !uiState.isLoadingMore) {
    56→            viewModel.loadMore()
    57→        }
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    Scaffold(
    61→        contentWindowInsets = WindowInsets(0),
    62→        topBar = {
    63→            CompactTopBar(title = stringResource(R.string.personal_timeline))
    64→        },
    65→        floatingActionButton = {
    66→            FloatingActionButton(
    67→                onClick = { onComposeClick(null) },
    68→                containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary
    69→            ) {
    70→                Icon(
    71→                    imageVector = Icons.Default.Add,
    72→                    contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.compose)
    73→                )
    74→            }
    75→        }
    76→    ) { paddingValues ->
    77→        Box(
    78→            modifier = Modifier
    79→                .fillMaxSize()
    80→                .padding(paddingValues)
    81→        ) {
    82→            when {
    83→                uiState.isLoading && uiState.posts.isEmpty() -> {
    84→                    FullScreenLoading()
    85→                }
    86→                uiState.error != null && uiState.posts.isEmpty() -> {
    87→                    ErrorMessage(
    88→                        message = uiState.error ?: stringResource(R.string.error_generic),
    89→                        onRetry = { viewModel.refresh() }
    90→                    )
    91→                }
    92→                uiState.posts.isEmpty() -> {
    93→                    ErrorMessage(
    94→                        message = stringResource(R.string.no_posts)
    95→                    )
    96→                }
    97→                else -> {
    98→                    PullToRefreshBox(
    99→                        isRefreshing = uiState.isRefreshing,
   100→                        onRefresh = { viewModel.refresh() }

1→package pub.hackers.android.ui.screens.compose
     2→
     3→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Box

    23→import androidx.compose.material.icons.outlined.Group
    24→import androidx.compose.material.icons.outlined.Lock
    25→import androidx.compose.material3.Button
    26→import androidx.compose.material3.CircularProgressIndicator
    27→import androidx.compose.material3.DropdownMenu
    28→import androidx.compose.material3.DropdownMenuItem

    34→import androidx.compose.material3.SnackbarHost
    35→import androidx.compose.material3.SnackbarHostState
    36→import androidx.compose.material3.Surface
    37→import androidx.compose.material3.Text
    38→import androidx.compose.material3.TextButton
    39→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.CompactTopBar
    40→import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
    41→import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
    42→import androidx.compose.runtime.collectAsState
    43→import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
    44→import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableIntStateOf
    45→import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableStateOf
    46→import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
    47→import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
    48→import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
    49→import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
    50→import androidx.compose.ui.draw.alpha
    51→import androidx.compose.ui.draw.clip
    52→import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Rect
    53→import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.SolidColor
    54→import androidx.compose.ui.layout.ContentScale
    55→import androidx.compose.ui.layout.boundsInWindow
    56→import androidx.compose.ui.layout.onGloballyPositioned
    57→import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalDensity
    58→import androidx.compose.ui.res.stringResource
    59→import androidx.compose.ui.text.TextLayoutResult
    60→import androidx.compose.ui.text.TextRange
    61→import androidx.compose.ui.text.input.TextFieldValue
    62→import androidx.compose.ui.text.style.TextOverflow
    63→import androidx.compose.ui.unit.IntOffset
    64→import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
    65→import androidx.compose.ui.window.Popup
    66→import androidx.compose.ui.window.PopupProperties
    67→import androidx.hilt.navigation.compose.hiltViewModel
    68→import coil.compose.AsyncImage
    69→import pub.hackers.android.R
    70→import pub.hackers.android.domain.model.Post
    71→import pub.hackers.android.domain.model.PostVisibility
    72→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.HtmlContent
    73→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.MentionAutocomplete
    74→import kotlin.math.roundToInt
    75→
    76→@Composable
    77→fun ComposeScreen(
    78→    replyToId: String?,
    79→    onPostSuccess: () -> Unit,
    80→    onNavigateBack: () -> Unit,
    81→    viewModel: ComposeViewModel = hiltViewModel()
    82→) {
    83→    val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsState()
    84→    val snackbarHostState = remember { SnackbarHostState() }
    85→    var showVisibilityMenu by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
    86→
    87→    // Track TextFieldValue for cursor position
    88→    var textFieldValue by remember {
    89→        mutableStateOf(TextFieldValue(text = uiState.content))
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    // Track cursor and text field positions for popup placement
    93→    var cursorRect by remember { mutableStateOf(Rect.Zero) }
    94→    var textFieldBounds by remember { mutableStateOf(Rect.Zero) }
    95→    var textLayoutResult by remember { mutableStateOf<TextLayoutResult?>(null) }
    96→    val scrollState = rememberScrollState()
    97→    val density = LocalDensity.current
    98→    val popupHeight = with(density) { 200.dp.toPx() } // Estimated popup height
    99→
   100→    // Sync TextFieldValue with ViewModel state changes (e.g., when mention is selected)
   101→    LaunchedEffect(uiState.content, uiState.cursorPosition) {
   102→        if (textFieldValue.text != uiState.content) {
   103→            textFieldValue = TextFieldValue(
   104→                text = uiState.content,
   105→                selection = TextRange(uiState.cursorPosition)
   106→            )
   107→        }
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    LaunchedEffect(replyToId) {
   111→        replyToId?.let { viewModel.setReplyTarget(it) }
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    LaunchedEffect(uiState.isPosted) {
   115→        if (uiState.isPosted) {
   116→            onPostSuccess()
   117→        }
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    LaunchedEffect(uiState.error) {
   121→        uiState.error?.let {
   122→            snackbarHostState.showSnackbar(it)
   123→            viewModel.clearError()
   124→        }
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    Scaffold(
   128→        contentWindowInsets = WindowInsets(0),
   129→        topBar = {
   130→            CompactTopBar(
   131→                title = if (replyToId != null) stringResource(R.string.reply) else stringResource(R.string.compose),
   132→                navigationIcon = {
   133→                    IconButton(onClick = onNavigateBack) {
   134→                        Icon(
   135→                            imageVector = Icons.Default.Close,
   136→                            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.cancel)
   137→                        )
   138→                    }
   139→                },
   140→                actions = {
   141→                    Button(
   142→                        onClick = { viewModel.post() },
   143→                        enabled = uiState.content.isNotBlank() && !uiState.isPosting
   144→                    ) {
   145→                        Text(stringResource(R.string.post))
   146→                    }
   147→                }
   148→            )
   149→        },
   150→        snackbarHost = { SnackbarHost(snackbarHostState) }

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary of my findings:

## Comprehensive Exploration Summary: Article vs Note Rendering

I've thoroughly explored the hackerspub-web-next project and the parent hackerspub GraphQL schema. Here are the key findings about how articles are rendered differently from notes:

### 1. **Article Card Component** (`/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub/web-next/src/components/ArticleCard.tsx`)

**Visual/Structural Differences:**
- **Article cards are full-width standalone cards** with distinct styling:
  - Flex column layout with `flex-col` class
  - Bottom border with last card rounded bottom: `border-b last:border-none last:rounded-b-xl`
  - Full-width "Read full article" button at the bottom with accent styling
  - Background changes on hover: `bg-accent` 

- **Article card contains multiple sections:**
  - Author section (avatar + name/handle + timestamp) with larger styling (size-12 avatar)
  - Prominent article title displayed as `<h1>` with `text-xl font-semibold`
  - Optional LLM-generated summary with special styling (label "Summarized by LLM", border, background)
  - If no summary, displays truncated HTML content with `line-clamp-4 overflow-hidden`
  - "Read full article" call-to-action button at the bottom

- **Support for shared articles:**
  - Can display a `PostSharer` wrapper showing who shared the article
  - Shows `sharedPost` content with original article details

### 2. **Note Card Component** (`/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub/web-next/src/components/NoteCard.tsx` & `NoteCardInternal.tsx`)

**Visual/Structural Differences:**
- **Much more compact design** using inline content model:
  - Single article wrapper with `px-4 py-2 border-b-1` (tight padding)
  - Horizontal flex layout with avatar on left and content on right
  - Includes author avatar, header, content, media, link preview, quoted post
  - **PostControls** button bar showing: reply, quote, share, reactions counts
  - No title field
  - Content rendered directly as prose HTML

### 3. **Key Field Differences in GraphQL Schema**

**Article has additional fields not on Note:**

| Field | Article | Note | Purpose |
|-------|---------|------|---------|
| `name` | Yes | Yes | Article title |
| `slug` | Yes (Article-specific) | No | URL-friendly identifier |
| `publishedYear` | Yes (Article-specific) | No | Publication year |
| `tags` | Yes (Article-specific) | No | Article tags |
| `contents` | Yes (Article-specific) | No | Multi-language article versions with translations |
| `allowLlmTranslation` | Yes (Article-specific) | No | AI translation permission flag |
| `account` | Yes (Article has it explicitly) | No | Article author account info |
| `excerpt` | Both | Both | Summary/excerpt |
| `summary` | Both | Both | Summary field |

**ArticleContent is a unique type** containing:
- Language-specific content (title, summary, HTML content)
- Original language tracking for translations
- Translation status and translator info
- Generated URL with pattern: `/@username/year/slug/language`

### 4. **Article-Specific Routes**

- **Feed route:** `/feed/articles` - filters PersonalTimeline by `postType: ARTICLE`
- **Profile articles:** `/@handle/articles` - shows all articles by a user
- **Article drafts:** `/@handle/drafts/` - create, edit, publish articles
- **No individual article detail page found** - articles appear to link externally via their `url` field

### 5. **Rendering Logic Differentiation**

**PostCard component** (`/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub/web-next/src/components/PostCard.tsx`):
```typescript
// Dispatches based on __typename
switch (post().__typename) {
  case "Note": return <NoteCard />
  case "Article": return <ArticleCard />
}
```

The system uses **union types** to distinguish posts:
- GraphQL schema defines `Post` as an interface implemented by `Note`, `Article`, and `Question`
- Runtime type checking via `__typename` field

### 6. **Visual Styling Differences Summary**

| Aspect | Article | Note |
|--------|---------|------|
| Layout | Full-width card sections | Compact inline with avatar sidebar |
| Title Treatment | Prominent h1 heading | Optional field, not featured |
| Summary Display | Special LLM label and styling | Part of content flow |
| Metadata | Large timestamp/author header | Compact header with visibility tag |
| Call-to-Action | "Read full article" button | PostControls (reply/quote/share/react) |
| Content Display | Truncated or full via external link | Full markdown content shown |
| Media | List format | Integrated with content |
| Interactions | Minimalist (share indicator) | Full engagement controls |
| Padding/Spacing | Generous (p-4) | Tight (px-4 py-2) |

### 7. **Article Composer Components**

Articles have a dedicated composition system:
- `/src/components/article-composer/` directory
- Separate from note composition
- Handles drafts with title, content, tags
- Publish workflow with slug and language selection

### 8. **Key Design Pattern**

The system uses a **discriminated union pattern**:
1. All posts have `__typename` field
2. UI components check `__typename` to render appropriate card type
3. PersonalTimeline filters by `postType` enum: `ARTICLE | NOTE | QUESTION`
4. ArticleCard and NoteCard have completely different layouts, not variations of the same component

This exploration shows that articles and notes are fundamentally different content types with distinct visual presentations, data models, and interaction patterns. Articles prioritize readability and external linking, while notes prioritize direct engagement through social interactions.

- **No individual article detail page found** - articles appear to link externally via their `url` field

### 5. **Rendering Logic Differentiation**

**PostCard component** (`/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub/web-next/src/components/PostCard.tsx`):
```typescript
// Dispatches based on __typename
switch (post().__typename) {
  case "Note": return <NoteCard />
  case "Article": return <ArticleCard />
}
```

The system uses **union types** to distinguish posts:
- GraphQL schema defines `Post` as an interface implemented by `Note`, `Article`, and `Question`
- Runtime type checking via `__typename` field

### 6. **Visual Styling Differences Summary**

| Aspect | Article | Note |
|--------|---------|------|
| Layout | Full-width card sections | Compact inline with avatar sidebar |
| Title Treatment | Prominent h1 heading | Optional field, not featured |
| Summary Display | Special LLM label and styling | Part of content flow |
| Metadata | Large timestamp/author header | Compact header with visibility tag |
| Call-to-Action | "Read full article" button | PostControls (reply/quote/share/react) |
| Content Display | Truncated or full via external link | Full markdown content shown |
| Media | List format | Integrated with content |
| Interactions | Minimalist (share indicator) | Full engagement controls |
| Padding/Spacing | Generous (p-4) | Tight (px-4 py-2) |

### 7. **Article Composer Components**

Articles have a dedicated composition system:
- `/src/components/article-composer/` directory
- Separate from note composition
- Handles drafts with title, content, tags
- Publish workflow with slug and language selection

### 8. **Key Design Pattern**

The system uses a **discriminated union pattern**:
1. All posts have `__typename` field
2. UI components check `__typename` to render appropriate card type
3. PersonalTimeline filters by `postType` enum: `ARTICLE | NOTE | QUESTION`
4. ArticleCard and NoteCard have completely different layouts, not variations of the same component

This exploration shows that articles and notes are fundamentally different content types with distinct visual presentations, data models, and interaction patterns. Articles prioritize readability and external linking, while notes prioritize direct engagement through social interactions.

```kotlin
sealed class DetailScreen(val route: String) {

    }
    data object Profile : DetailScreen("profile/{handle}") {
        fun createRoute(handle: String) = "profile/$handle"
    }
    data object Compose : DetailScreen("compose?replyTo={replyTo}") {
        fun createRoute(replyTo: String? = null) =
            if (replyTo != null) "compose?replyTo=$replyTo" else "compose"
    }
    data object SignIn : DetailScreen("signin")
}

sealed class Screen(val route: String) {
    data object Timeline : Screen("timeline")
    data object Notifications : Screen("notifications")
    data object Explore : Screen("explore")
    data object Search : Screen("search")
    data object Settings : Screen("settings")
}
```

#### **Navigation Graph (Composable)**
The `NavHost` defines navigation across all screens (lines 158-303):

```kotlin
NavHost(
    navController = navController,
    startDestination = if (isLoggedIn) Screen.Timeline.route else Screen.Explore.route,
    modifier = Modifier.padding(innerPadding)
) {
    composable(Screen.Timeline.route) { TimelineScreen(...) }
    composable(Screen.Notifications.route) { NotificationsScreen(...) }
    composable(Screen.Explore.route) { ExploreScreen(...) }
    composable(Screen.Search.route) { SearchScreen(...) }
    composable(Screen.Settings.route) { SettingsScreen(...) }
    
    composable(DetailScreen.PostDetail.route, arguments = listOf(...)) {
        PostDetailScreen(
            postId = postId,
            onNavigateBack = { navController.popBackStack() },
            onProfileClick = { handle -> 
                navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
            },
            onReplyClick = { id ->
                navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(id))
            },
            onPostClick = { id ->
                navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(id))
            }
        )
    }
    
    composable(DetailScreen.Profile.route, arguments = listOf(...)) {
        ProfileScreen(...)
    }
    
    composable(
        route = DetailScreen.Compose.route,
        arguments = listOf(navArgument("replyTo") { ... })
    ) {
        ComposeScreen(
            replyToId = replyTo,
            onPostSuccess = { navController.popBackStack() },
            onNavigateBack = { navController.popBackStack() }
        )
    }
}
```

#### **Navigation Callbacks**
All screens receive callbacks to navigate:
- `onPostClick(postId: String)` - Navigate to post detail
- `onProfileClick(handle: String)` - Navigate to profile
- `onReplyClick(postId: String)` - Navigate to compose with reply target
- `onNavigateBack()` - Pop the back stack

### 6. **Full Post Model Structure**

**From Models.kt:**
```kotlin
data class Post(
    val id: String,                    // GraphQL ID
    val typename: String,              // "__typename" from GraphQL (e.g., "Article", "Note")
    val name: String?,                 // Title/name (articles usually have this)
    val published: Instant,            // Creation timestamp
    val summary: String?,              // Optional summary/excerpt
    val content: String,               // HTML content
    val excerpt: String,               // Short excerpt for preview
    val url: String?,                  // External URL
    val viewerHasShared: Boolean,      // Has current user shared this?
    val actor: Actor,                  // Author
    val media: List<Media>,            // Attached media (images, videos, etc.)
    val engagementStats: EngagementStats,  // Reactions, replies, shares, quotes
    val mentions: List<String>,        // Mentioned usernames
    val sharedPost: Post? = null,      // If this is a repost, the original post
    val replyTarget: Post? = null,     // If this is a reply, the post being replied to
    val quotedPost: Post? = null,      // If this quotes another post
    val visibility: PostVisibility = PostVisibility.PUBLIC  // PUBLIC, UNLISTED, FOLLOWERS, DIRECT, NONE
)

data class Actor(
    val id: String,
    val name: String?,
    val handle: String,
    val avatarUrl: String
)

data class Media(
    val url: String,
    val thumbnailUrl: String?,
    val alt: String?,
    val height: Int?,
    val width: Int?
)

data class EngagementStats(
    val replies: Int,
    val reactions: Int,
    val shares: Int,
    val quotes: Int
)
```

### 7. **Data Transformation Layer**

In `HackersPubRepository.kt`, GraphQL responses are mapped to domain models:

**PostFields Fragment to Post Mapping** (lines 474-498):
```kotlin
private fun PostFields.toPost(
    sharedPost: Post? = null,
    replyTarget: Post? = null,
    visibility: PostVisibility = PostVisibility.PUBLIC
): Post {
    return Post(
        id = id,
        typename = __typename,          // <- Captures "Article" or "Note"
        name = name,                    // <- Title from GraphQL
        published = Instant.parse(published.toString()),
        summary = summary,
        content = content.toString(),
        excerpt = excerpt,
        url = url?.toString(),
        viewerHasShared = viewerHasShared,
        actor = actor.actorFields.toActor(),
        media = media.map { it.mediaFields.toMedia() },
        engagementStats = engagementStats.engagementStatsFields.toEngagementStats(),
        mentions = mentions.edges.map { it.node.handle },
        sharedPost = sharedPost,
        replyTarget = replyTarget,
        quotedPost = quotedPost?.sharedPostFields?.toPost(),
        visibility = visibility
    )
}
```

---

The app fully supports replies:

- `replyToId: String?` parameter passed to ComposeScreen

- Replies fetched in `PostDetail` query

```kotlin
    val sharedPost: Post? = null,      // If this is a repost, the original post
    val replyTarget: Post? = null,     // If this is a reply, the post being replied to
    val quotedPost: Post? = null,      // If this quotes another post
    val visibility: PostVisibility = PostVisibility.PUBLIC  // PUBLIC, UNLISTED, FOLLOWERS, DIRECT, NONE
)

data class Actor(
    val id: String,
    val name: String?,
    val handle: String,
    val avatarUrl: String
)

data class Media(
    val url: String,
    val thumbnailUrl: String?,
    val alt: String?,
    val height: Int?,
    val width: Int?
)

data class EngagementStats(
    val replies: Int,
    val reactions: Int,
    val shares: Int,
    val quotes: Int
)
```

---

### 7. **Data Transformation Layer**

In `HackersPubRepository.kt`, GraphQL responses are mapped to domain models:

**PostFields Fragment to Post Mapping** (lines 474-498):
```kotlin
private fun PostFields.toPost(
    sharedPost: Post? = null,
    replyTarget: Post? = null,
    visibility: PostVisibility = PostVisibility.PUBLIC
): Post {
    return Post(
        id = id,
        typename = __typename,          // <- Captures "Article" or "Note"
        name = name,                    // <- Title from GraphQL
        published = Instant.parse(published.toString()),
        summary = summary,
        content = content.toString(),
        excerpt = excerpt,
        url = url?.toString(),
        viewerHasShared = viewerHasShared,
        actor = actor.actorFields.toActor(),
        media = media.map { it.mediaFields.toMedia() },
        engagementStats = engagementStats.engagementStatsFields.toEngagementStats(),
        mentions = mentions.edges.map { it.node.handle },
        sharedPost = sharedPost,
        replyTarget = replyTarget,
        quotedPost = quotedPost?.sharedPostFields?.toPost(),
        visibility = visibility
    )
}
```

---

### 8. **Feature: Reply Functionality**

The app fully supports replies:

#### **In Compose Flow** (ComposeScreen.kt)
- `replyToId: String?` parameter passed to ComposeScreen
- Displays reply target preview when replying
- `viewModel.setReplyTarget(id)` loads the post being replied to

#### **In Post Detail** (PostDetailScreen.kt)
- Displays `post.replyTarget` if the post is a reply
- Users can reply to the post via FloatingActionButton
- Shows reply preview with quoted post component

#### **GraphQL Support**
- `replyTarget: Post` field in Post interface
- `CreateNoteMutation` includes `replyTargetId` parameter
- Replies fetched in `PostDetail` query

---

### **Summary Table**

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Type Differentiation** | `typename` field in Post model (stores "Article" or "Note") |
| **Post Types** | Article, Note, Question (in GraphQL schema) |
| **Title Field** | `name: String?` in Post (articles typically have this) |
| **Content Fields** | `content` (HTML), `summary` (optional), `excerpt` (preview) |
| **PostCard Rendering** | Currently treats all types identically, no type-specific styling |
| **Navigation Routes** | post/{postId}, profile/{handle}, compose?replyTo={replyTo}, signin |
| **Key Navigation Screens** | Timeline, Notifications, Explore, Search, Settings, PostDetail, Profile, Compose |
| **Reply Support** | Full support via replyTarget field and CreateNoteMutation |
| **Threading Support** | replyTarget (reply), quotedPost (quote), sharedPost (share/repost) |
| **Media Support** | Media array with url, thumbnailUrl, alt, dimensions |
| **Engagement Stats** | replies, reactions, shares, quotes |

---

**All absolute file paths:**
- `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/domain/model/Models.kt`
- `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/graphql/pub/hackers/android/schema.graphqls`
- `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/graphql/pub/hackers/android/operations.graphql`
- `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/components/PostCard.kt`
- `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/HackersPubApp.kt`
- `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/data/repository/HackersPubRepository.kt`

Let me now design the plan. Here's what I need to do:

### Article Card (Timeline view)
Based on the web-next ArticleCard:
1. Full-width card layout (no avatar sidebar like notes)
2. Author section at top with larger avatar (48dp -> maybe keep 48dp)
3. Prominent title as heading (`text-xl font-semibold`)
4. Summary or truncated content below title
5. "Read full article" button at bottom with muted background


6. No engagement bar (unlike notes)

### Article Detail Page
The web-next doesn't have a separate article detail page - articles link to their URL. But for the Android app, I need a proper detail view. I should create an ArticleDetailScreen that shows the full article content as rendered HTML, displays author info and title prominently, includes engagement stats and reactions, and provides reply/share actions.

For the implementation approach, I'm considering whether to create a separate ArticleCard composable and modify PostCard to dispatch to it, or handle articles within the existing post structure.

Option A feels cleaner since articles and notes are fundamentally different content types based on how the web-next codebase treats them. But I'm reconsidering the detail page itself - the web version just links to the article URL on hackers.pub, so for Android I could either open it in a browser or build an in-app reader. Since the user specifically asked for an article detail page, they likely want the in-app experience.

For navigation, I can reuse the existing `PostDetail` route and have `PostDetailScreen` check the `typename` to render articles differently - that keeps all existing navigation working without adding new routes. Let me finalize the approach: create an `ArticleCard` composable for the timeline, modify `PostDetailScreen` to handle article rendering with full content and larger typography, and add a button to open the article URL in a browser if needed.

  - Prominent article title (text-xl font-semibold)
  - Summary text (or truncated content if no summary, 4 lines max)
  - "Read full article" button at the bottom with muted background, border-top
  - No engagement bar (unlike notes which have reply/quote/share/react controls)

- Notes (NoteCardInternal.tsx) have:
  - Compact layout with avatar sidebar
  - Content with full HTML rendering
  - PostControls bar (reply, quote, share, reactions)

- PostCard.tsx dispatches: `Switch` on `__typename === "Note"` vs `"Article"`

   152→            )
   153→
   154→            if (displayPost.media.isNotEmpty()) {
   155→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   156→                MediaGrid(media = displayPost.media)
   157→            }
   158→
   159→            if (displayPost.quotedPost != null) {
   160→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   161→                QuotedPostPreview(
   162→                    post = displayPost.quotedPost!!,
   163→                    onClick = { onQuotedPostClick?.invoke(displayPost.quotedPost!!.id) },
   164→                    onProfileClick = onProfileClick
   165→                )
   166→            }
   167→
   168→            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(12.dp))
   169→
   170→            EngagementBar(
   171→                post = displayPost,
   172→                onReplyClick = onReplyClick,
   173→                onShareClick = onShareClick
   174→            )
   175→        }
   176→    }
   177→}
   178→
   179→@Composable
   180→private fun EngagementBar(
   181→    post: Post,
   182→    onReplyClick: (() -> Unit)?,
   183→    onShareClick: (() -> Unit)?
   184→) {
   185→    Row(
   186→        modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   187→        horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceBetween
   188→    ) {
   189→        EngagementButton(
   190→            icon = Icons.Outlined.ChatBubbleOutline,
   191→            count = post.engagementStats.replies,
   192→            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.replies),
   193→            onClick = onReplyClick
   194→        )
   195→
   196→        EngagementButton(
   197→            icon = if (post.viewerHasShared) Icons.Filled.Repeat else Icons.Outlined.Repeat,
   198→            count = post.engagementStats.shares,
   199→            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.shares),
   200→            onClick = onShareClick,
   201→            isActive = post.viewerHasShared
   202→        )
   203→
   204→        EngagementButton(
   205→            icon = Icons.Outlined.Favorite,
   206→            count = post.engagementStats.reactions,
   207→            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.reactions),
   208→            onClick = null
   209→        )
   210→
   211→        EngagementButton(
   212→            icon = Icons.Outlined.FormatQuote,
   213→            count = post.engagementStats.quotes,
   214→            contentDescription = stringResource(R.string.quotes),
   215→            onClick = null
   216→        )
   217→    }
   218→}
   219→
   220→@Composable
   221→private fun EngagementButton(
   222→    icon: androidx.compose.ui.graphics.vector.ImageVector,
   223→    count: Int,
   224→    contentDescription: String,
   225→    onClick: (() -> Unit)?,
   226→    isActive: Boolean = false
   227→) {
   228→    Row(
   229→        verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
   230→    ) {
   231→        IconButton(
   232→            onClick = { onClick?.invoke() },
   233→            enabled = onClick != null
   234→        ) {
   235→            Icon(
   236→                imageVector = icon,
   237→                contentDescription = contentDescription,
   238→                tint = if (isActive) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   239→                modifier = Modifier.size(20.dp)
   240→            )
   241→        }
   242→        if (count > 0) {
   243→            Text(
   244→                text = formatCount(count),
   245→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   246→                color = if (isActive) MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary else MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   247→            )
   248→        }
   249→    }
   250→}
   251→
   252→@Composable
   253→fun QuotedPostPreview(
   254→    post: Post,
   255→    onClick: () -> Unit,
   256→    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit
   257→) {
   258→    Surface(
   259→        modifier = Modifier
   260→            .fillMaxWidth()
   261→            .clickable(onClick = onClick),
   262→        shape = RoundedCornerShape(12.dp),
   263→        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surfaceVariant.copy(alpha = 0.5f),
   264→        border = BorderStroke(
   265→            1.dp,
   266→            MaterialTheme.colorScheme.outlineVariant
   267→        )
   268→    ) {
   269→        Column(
   270→            modifier = Modifier.padding(12.dp)
   271→        ) {
   272→            Row(
   273→                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
   274→            ) {
   275→                AsyncImage(
   276→                    model = post.actor.avatarUrl,
   277→                    contentDescription = null,
   278→                    modifier = Modifier
   279→                        .size(20.dp)
   280→                        .clip(CircleShape)
   281→                        .clickable { onProfileClick(post.actor.handle) },
   282→                    contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   283→                )
   284→
   285→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(8.dp))
   286→
   287→                Text(
   288→                    text = post.actor.name ?: post.actor.handle,
   289→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   290→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold,
   291→                    maxLines = 1,
   292→                    overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis,
   293→                    modifier = Modifier
   294→                        .weight(1f, fill = false)
   295→                        .clickable { onProfileClick(post.actor.handle) }
   296→                )
   297→
   298→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(4.dp))
   299→
   300→                Text(
   301→                    text = "@${post.actor.handle}",
   302→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   303→                    color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
   304→                    maxLines = 1,
   305→                    overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis,
   306→                    modifier = Modifier.weight(1f, fill = false)
   307→                )
   308→            }
   309→
   310→            Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   311→
   312→            post.name?.let { title ->
   313→                Text(
   314→                    text = title,
   315→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodySmall,
   316→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold,
   317→                    maxLines = 1,
   318→                    overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis,
   319→                    modifier = Modifier.padding(bottom = 4.dp)
   320→                )
   321→            }
   322→
   323→            HtmlContent(
   324→                html = post.content,
   325→                maxLines = 3,
   326→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   327→                onMentionClick = onProfileClick
   328→            )
   329→
   330→            if (post.media.isNotEmpty()) {
   331→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(8.dp))
   332→                AsyncImage(
   333→                    model = post.media.first().url,
   334→                    contentDescription = post.media.first().alt,
   335→                    modifier = Modifier
   336→                        .fillMaxWidth()
   337→                        .height(120.dp)
   338→                        .clip(RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)),
   339→                    contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   340→                )
   341→            }
   342→        }
   343→    }
   344→}
   345→
   346→@Composable
   347→fun MediaGrid(media: List<pub.hackers.android.domain.model.Media>) {
   348→    when (media.size) {
   349→        1 -> {
   350→            AsyncImage(
   351→                model = media[0].url,
   352→                contentDescription = media[0].alt,
   353→                modifier = Modifier
   354→                    .fillMaxWidth()
   355→                    .height(200.dp)
   356→                    .clip(RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)),
   357→                contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   358→            )
   359→        }
   360→        2 -> {
   361→            Row(
   362→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   363→            ) {
   364→                media.forEach { item ->
   365→                    AsyncImage(
   366→                        model = item.url,
   367→                        contentDescription = item.alt,
   368→                        modifier = Modifier
   369→                            .weight(1f)
   370→                            .height(150.dp)
   371→                            .clip(RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)),
   372→                        contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   373→                    )
   374→                }
   375→            }
   376→        }
   377→        else -> {
   378→            Column(
   379→                verticalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   380→            ) {
   381→                Row(
   382→                    horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   383→                ) {
   384→                    media.take(2).forEach { item ->
   385→                        AsyncImage(
   386→                            model = item.url,
   387→                            contentDescription = item.alt,
   388→                            modifier = Modifier
   389→                                .weight(1f)
   390→                                .height(100.dp)
   391→                                .clip(RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)),
   392→                            contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   393→                        )
   394→                    }
   395→                }
   396→                if (media.size > 2) {
   397→                    Row(
   398→                        horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.spacedBy(4.dp)
   399→                    ) {
   400→                        media.drop(2).take(2).forEach { item ->
   401→                            AsyncImage(
   402→                                model = item.url,
   403→                                contentDescription = item.alt,
   404→                                modifier = Modifier
   405→                                    .weight(1f)
   406→                                    .height(100.dp)
   407→                                    .clip(RoundedCornerShape(12.dp)),
   408→                                contentScale = ContentScale.Crop
   409→                            )
   410→                        }
   411→                    }
   412→                }
   413→            }
   414→        }
   415→    }
   416→}
   417→
   418→
   419→private fun formatRelativeTime(instant: Instant): String {
   420→    val now = Instant.now()
   421→    val duration = Duration.between(instant, now)
   422→
   423→    return when {
   424→        duration.toMinutes() < 1 -> "now"
   425→        duration.toMinutes() < 60 -> "${duration.toMinutes()}m"
   426→        duration.toHours() < 24 -> "${duration.toHours()}h"
   427→        duration.toDays() < 7 -> "${duration.toDays()}d"
   428→        duration.toDays() < 30 -> "${duration.toDays() / 7}w"
   429→        duration.toDays() < 365 -> "${duration.toDays() / 30}mo"
   430→        else -> "${duration.toDays() / 365}y"
   431→    }
   432→}
   433→
   434→private fun formatCount(count: Int): String {
   435→    return when {
   436→        count < 1000 -> count.toString()
   437→        count < 1_000_000 -> String.format("%.1fK", count / 1000.0)
   438→        else -> String.format("%.1fM", count / 1_000_000.0)
   439→    }
   440→}
   441→

data.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for adding article-specific rendering to the hackerspub-android app. Here's the context:

**Current state:**
- `PostCard.kt` renders all post types (Article, Note, Question) identically
- `PostDetailScreen.kt` is the detail view, also treats all types the same
- The `Post` model has `typename` field ("Article" or "Note") and `name` (title), `summary`, `content`, `excerpt`, `url` fields
- Navigation uses `DetailScreen.PostDetail` route with postId

**Web-next reference (ArticleCard.tsx):**
- Articles have a distinct card in the timeline:
  - Full-width layout (no avatar sidebar like notes)
  - Author section at top: avatar (48px) + name + handle + timestamp
  - Prominent article title (text-xl font-semibold)
  - Summary text (or truncated content if no summary, 4 lines max)
  - "Read full article" button at the bottom with muted background, border-top
  - No engagement bar (unlike notes which have reply/quote/share/react controls)

- Notes (NoteCardInternal.tsx) have:
  - Compact layout with avatar sidebar
  - Content with full HTML rendering
  - PostControls bar (reply, quote, share, reactions)

- PostCard.tsx dispatches: `Switch` on `__typename === "Note"` vs `"Article"`

**Requirements:**
1. Create an ArticleCard composable for timeline rendering with distinct article styling
2. Modify PostCard to dispatch between ArticleCard and NoteCard based on typename
3. Create/modify article detail page for full reading experience

**File paths:**
- PostCard: `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/components/PostCard.kt`
- PostDetailScreen: `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/screens/postdetail/PostDetailScreen.kt`
- HackersPubApp (nav): `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/HackersPubApp.kt`
- Models: `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/domain/model/Models.kt`
- HtmlContent: `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/components/HtmlContent.kt`

Design a concrete plan with specific file changes. Consider:
- Should ArticleCard be a new file or added to PostCard.kt?
- Should the article detail view reuse PostDetailScreen with conditional rendering or be a separate screen?
- How to handle the "Read full article" button (open URL in browser vs in-app WebView)
- What the article detail page should look like (full HTML content rendering, title, author info, engagement stats)

Keep it practical and minimal - don't over-engineer.

1→package pub.hackers.android.domain.model
     2→
     3→import java.time.Instant
     4→
     5→data class Actor(
     6→    val id: String,
     7→    val name: String?,
     8→    val handle: String,
     9→    val avatarUrl: String
    10→)
    11→
    12→data class Media(
    13→    val url: String,
    14→    val thumbnailUrl: String?,
    15→    val alt: String?,
    16→    val height: Int?,
    17→    val width: Int?
    18→)
    19→
    20→data class EngagementStats(
    21→    val replies: Int,
    22→    val reactions: Int,
    23→    val shares: Int,
    24→    val quotes: Int
    25→)
    26→
    27→data class Post(
    28→    val id: String,
    29→    val typename: String,
    30→    val name: String?,
    31→    val published: Instant,
    32→    val summary: String?,
    33→    val content: String,
    34→    val excerpt: String,
    35→    val url: String?,
    36→    val viewerHasShared: Boolean,
    37→    val actor: Actor,
    38→    val media: List<Media>,
    39→    val engagementStats: EngagementStats,
    40→    val mentions: List<String>,
    41→    val sharedPost: Post? = null,
    42→    val replyTarget: Post? = null,
    43→    val quotedPost: Post? = null,
    44→    val visibility: PostVisibility = PostVisibility.PUBLIC
    45→)
    46→
    47→enum class PostVisibility {
    48→    PUBLIC, UNLISTED, FOLLOWERS, DIRECT, NONE
    49→}
    50→
    51→sealed class Notification {
    52→    abstract val id: String
    53→    abstract val uuid: String
    54→    abstract val created: Instant
    55→    abstract val actors: List<Actor>
    56→
    57→    data class Follow(
    58→        override val id: String,
    59→        override val uuid: String,
    60→        override val created: Instant,
    61→        override val actors: List<Actor>
    62→    ) : Notification()
    63→
    64→    data class Mention(
    65→        override val id: String,
    66→        override val uuid: String,
    67→        override val created: Instant,
    68→        override val actors: List<Actor>,
    69→        val post: Post?
    70→    ) : Notification()
    71→
    72→    data class Reply(
    73→        override val id: String,
    74→        override val uuid: String,
    75→        override val created: Instant,
    76→        override val actors: List<Actor>,
    77→        val post: Post?
    78→    ) : Notification()
    79→
    80→    data class Quote(
    81→        override val id: String,
    82→        override val uuid: String,
    83→        override val created: Instant,
    84→        override val actors: List<Actor>,
    85→        val post: Post?
    86→    ) : Notification()
    87→
    88→    data class Share(
    89→        override val id: String,
    90→        override val uuid: String,
    91→        override val created: Instant,
    92→        override val actors: List<Actor>,
    93→        val post: Post?
    94→    ) : Notification()
    95→
    96→    data class React(
    97→        override val id: String,
    98→        override val uuid: String,
    99→        override val created: Instant,
   100→        override val actors: List<Actor>,
   101→        val emoji: String?,
   102→        val customEmoji: CustomEmoji?,
   103→        val post: Post?
   104→    ) : Notification()
   105→}
   106→
   107→data class CustomEmoji(
   108→    val id: String,
   109→    val name: String,
   110→    val imageUrl: String
   111→)
   112→
   113→data class ReactionGroup(
   114→    val emoji: String?,
   115→    val customEmoji: CustomEmoji?,
   116→    val count: Int,
   117→    val reactors: List<Actor>
   118→)
   119→
   120→data class Viewer(
   121→    val id: String,
   122→    val username: String,
   123→    val name: String,
   124→    val bio: String,
   125→    val avatarUrl: String,
   126→    val handle: String
   127→)
   128→
   129→data class LoginChallenge(
   130→    val token: String
   131→)
   132→
   133→data class Session(
   134→    val id: String,
   135→    val account: Account
   136→)
   137→
   138→data class Account(
   139→    val id: String,
   140→    val username: String,
   141→    val name: String,
   142→    val avatarUrl: String,
   143→    val handle: String
   144→)
   145→
   146→data class TimelineResult(
   147→    val posts: List<Post>,
   148→    val hasNextPage: Boolean,
   149→    val endCursor: String?
   150→)
   151→
   152→data class NotificationsResult(
   153→    val notifications: List<Notification>,
   154→    val hasNextPage: Boolean,
   155→    val endCursor: String?
   156→)
   157→
   158→data class PostDetailResult(
   159→    val post: Post,
   160→    val reactionGroups: List<ReactionGroup>,
   161→    val replies: List<Post>,
   162→    val hasMoreReplies: Boolean,
   163→    val repliesEndCursor: String?
   164→)
   165→
   166→data class ProfileResult(
   167→    val actor: Actor,
   168→    val bio: String?,
   169→    val posts: List<Post>,
   170→    val hasNextPage: Boolean,
   171→    val endCursor: String?
   172→)
   173→

1→package pub.hackers.android.ui.screens.postdetail
     2→

     4→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Box

     6→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Row

    11→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
    12→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.padding

    48→import pub.hackers.android.domain.model.ReactionGroup
    49→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.ErrorMessage
    50→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.FullScreenLoading
    51→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.HtmlContent
    52→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.MediaGrid
    53→import pub.hackers.android.ui.components.PostCard
